stories.headline: India Warns of Demand Hit to Economy After Iran War Boosts Costs
stories.summary: India is facing supply shocks from the Middle East war, and the resulting hit to domestic demand is a serious concern, the Finance Ministry said in its monthly economic review.

stories.headline: ‘No more Mr Nice Guy’: Trump warns Iran to ‘get smart’ over stalled talks
stories.summary: The US president has railed against Iran's proposal on Truth Social as uncertainty over the fragile ceasefire grows.

stories.headline: Trump warns Iran to ‘better get smart soon’ as US plans long port blockade
stories.summary: A second round of US-Iran peace talks was due to take place in Islamabad at the weekend, but Trump abruptly ordered his envoys not to attend.

stories.headline: King Charles Presents President With Submarine Bell From HMS Trump
stories.summary: The bell that King Charles III gave President Trump on his state visit came from a submarine that sank several Japanese ships in the Pacific War in 1945.

stories.headline: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
stories.summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

stories.headline: Ukraine formally asks Israel to seize vessel carrying grain stolen by Russia
stories.summary: A senior Ukrainian diplomatic source told Euronews that “if Israel responded to other requests via diplomatic channels, there would have been no need for tweets”, as Israeli FM Sa’ar lashed out at Kyiv over what he said was “Twitter diplomacy”.

stories.headline: Trump welcomes King Charles III and Queen Camilla for state dinner
stories.summary: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed Britain’s King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the White House for a state dinner in the East Room.

stories.summary: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOsThe volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them.Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing

stories.headline: EU backs ending new oil and gas drilling
stories.summary: The war in the Middle East may have driven up oil and gas demand, but the world should still ditch new fossil fuel drilling, EU climate chief Wopke Hoekstra has said, insisting on "affordable, reliable, homegrown clean energy."

stories.headline: Starbucks CEO Says Sales Momentum Is Continuing
stories.summary: Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol says more customers are coming in every day and they saw sales growth in every income cohort during the second quarter. He speaks to Bloomberg’s Romaine Bostick.

stories.headline: US Stocks Open Lower on Big Tech Losses Before Earnings Results

stories.headline: Australia Tells China Stable Trade Needs Stable Fuel Supply

stories.headline: Yen Hits Key 160-Per-Dollar Level as Traders Eye BOJ Next Steps
stories.summary: The yen slid past the key 160-per-dollar level as the currency traded under pressure following the Bank of Japan’s April meeting, at which Governor Kazuo Ueda refrained from providing a clear sign concerning the timing of the central bank’s next interest-rate hike.

stories.headline: Finland’s Kone Agrees to Buy TK Elevator for €29.4 Billion
stories.summary: Kone Oyj agreed to acquire TK Elevator for €29.4 billion ($34.4 billion) including debt, transforming the Finnish company into the industry’s dominant player and putting new pressure on its rivals.

stories.headline: White House Urges House to Quickly Fund D.H.S.
stories.summary: The call amounted to a rebuke of Speaker Mike Johnson, who has delayed action on a homeland security spending bill and suggested this week that it needed changes.

stories.headline: Sri Lanka’s government ‘temporarily’ takes over cricket board
stories.summary: Government says it will oversee the administrative functions of Sri Lanka Cricket until reforms are implemented.

stories.headline: Red card for players covering their mouths in World Cup matches
stories.summary: New red card rule allows referees to send off players who cover their mouths or walk off the pitch in protest against a decision.

stories.headline: ‘Encouraging’: Global rainforest loss slows in 2025 after record year
stories.summary: The researchers credit the slowdown to policies implemented by Brazil's President Lula da Silva to curb deforestation.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
stories.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: Bank Groups Eye Changes to Fed’s Relaxed Capital Plans
stories.summary: Bank groups said that although the Federal Reserve’s eased capital plans are a major improvement over previous versions, the recent proposals still need changes to help avoid risk assessments they say may hinder banks’ ability to boost lending.

stories.headline: Oil Rises as US-Iran Standoff Threatens Prolonged Supply Shock
stories.summary: Oil rose on signals that a US blockade on Iranian ports could go on for an extended period, prolonging an unprecedented supply shock that has roiled energy markets.

stories.headline: King Charles’s rare state visit offers U.K. a chance to mend ties with Trump
stories.summary: The pageantry began Monday amid heightened security concerns and a growing rift over the Iran war. The U.K. hopes the president’s love of pomp and the king’s “poker face” can help heal their alliance.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
